description: Protected classes under ECOA §701 as codified in the Reg B implementing rule. Direct-mention terms are hard block; combinatorial-proxy signals are advisory FLAG only because Fed itself has no crisp solution to combinatorial proxy detection. Red-team B1 defense: DO NOT claim this list solves combinatorial redlining; position as prophylactic §1002.6(b) enforcement + human review layer.

combinatorial_advisory_signals.description: Applicant ZIP prefix falls within an HMDA Majority-Minority Census Tract. Not by itself proof of proxy discrimination — real underwriting variance across geographies exists. But combined with other signals, flag for human review.
combinatorial_advisory_signals.reference: CFPB Ally $98M consent order (2013) established ZIP-based proxy detection framework

combinatorial_advisory_signals.description: Applicant surname falls in the top decile of SSA baby-name lists correlated with a specific race/ethnicity. Same caveat as ZIP — advisory only.
combinatorial_advisory_signals.reference: CFPB Bayesian Improved Surname Geocoding (BISG) methodology

combinatorial_advisory_signals.description: Non-English language preference recorded in application. Reg B §1002.4 requires bilingual disclosures if bank markets in that language; presence alone is not discriminatory.

honest_scope_disclosure: This scanner catches direct-mention protected class references in analyst prose (hard block) + surfaces the three most-cited combinatorial signals as advisory FLAG. It does NOT solve combinatorial proxy detection — the Fed itself has no crisp solution. Do NOT claim this list is exhaustive of ECOA proxy risk. Human review layer remains load-bearing per red-team B1 defense.
